overallInterpretation: The screenshot shows a rendered tilemap in the lower-left portion of the screen against a dark navy background. Multiple tile shapes are clearly visible: full square tiles (green, red, blue, gray), triangle tiles (diagonal half-cell fills in various colors including olive/dark-yellow, gray, and dark blue), and half tiles (rectangular shapes occupying partial cells). A red contour line traces the irregular outer boundary of the filled tile region, representing collision edges from carving — it steps around empty cells and follows triangle edges precisely. Small red diamond markers appear in some empty cells, likely additional collision/debug indicators. Faint gray grid lines overlay the tile area showing individual cell boundaries, and a larger dark red/brown rectangular outline is visible at the top-left representing a chunk boundary. All three expected elements — multiple tile shapes, red collision edges from carving, and debug overlays — are present and clearly rendered.

artifacts.interpretation: A tilemap rendered in the lower-left quadrant of the screen. Contains full square tiles, triangular tiles, and half tiles in multiple colors (green, red, blue, gray, olive/dark-yellow). A red outline traces the carved collision boundary of the tile region, following the irregular shape including triangle edges and carved-out empty cells. Faint gray grid lines show individual tile cell boundaries. A larger dark red/brown rectangle at the top-left indicates a chunk boundary. Small red diamond shapes mark points in some empty cells.
